home.h1Lead: Find out which channels actually drive
home.subtitle: The only WordPress plugin that does real multi-touch attribution for WooCommerce. See the actual impact of Google Ads, Meta, email and SEO on your sales, not just the last click.
home.trustLine: Free forever · No credit card · GDPR compliant
home.problemTitle: The last-click lie is costing you sales
home.problemLede: You're spending on ads but you don't know what's working. GA4 is lying to you, your platforms contradict each other, and your real winners stay invisible.

problems.b: GA4 credits everything to last-click. That Facebook ad that started the journey? Invisible.
Weeks to configure GA4, months for ad integrations. And it still works halfway.
Google Ads in one tab, Meta in another, your store elsewhere. You're drowning in dashboards.

home.solutionTitle: See the full customer journey in one place

features.b: Compare Last-touch, First-touch, Linear, Time-decay, Position-based, and Data-driven (ML). Same conversion, different credit. Pick the model that matches your reality.

features.h: Real revenue, not just page views
features.b: COGS, transaction fees, refunds, net profit. Not just gross sales. Calculated natively from WooCommerce, zero config.
Anonymized IP, email never stored in clear, no cookie banner required in most regions. PIPEDA, GDPR, CCPA ready.

features.h: From install to insights in under 5 minutes
features.b: Install the plugin, verify tracking, set your first goal, see data flow in. No GTM, no code, no consultants.

home.modelsLede: Each model distributes credit across touchpoints differently. Which one you pick changes which campaigns appear to "work".

models.desc: All credit to the last channel before conversion. Simple, but biased toward the end of the journey.
All credit to the first discovering channel. Reveals where your audience begins their journey.
models.when: When you want to optimize acquisition and brand awareness.

models.desc: Equal credit across all touchpoints. Most democratic view, but not intent-aware.
More credit to touches closer to conversion. Exponential decay.
models.when: For short journeys where every interaction counts.

models.desc: 40% first, 40% last, 20% middle. Balances discovery and closing.
models.when: When discovery and conversion matter equally.

models.desc: An algorithm that learns from your own data to attribute credit based on the real impact of each channel.
models.when: Recommended if you have more than 100 conversions/month.

wp.devLede: Hooks and filters in free. REST API and webhooks in Pro. All standard, documented WordPress patterns.

faq.q: Does it work with HPOS (High-Performance Order Storage)?
faq.a: Yes. Ajnix uses the WooCommerce Orders API, which is fully HPOS-compatible. Works on legacy posts-based stores too.

faq.q: Does it slow down my WooCommerce store?
faq.a: No. The JavaScript tracker is under 15 KB gzipped and loads asynchronously. No blocking DB queries: all aggregations run in background jobs (daily cron). Our test stores show an impact under 10 ms on LCP.

faq.q: Is my data sent to third-party servers?
faq.a: No on the free version. All data stays in your WordPress database. On Pro, you can opt into a sync to our SaaS servers for advanced features (consolidated dashboard, AI insights, server-side conversions). Explicit opt-in, never by default.

On uninstall, Ajnix offers two options: "Delete everything" (removes all tables and options from the database) or "Keep data" (deactivates the plugin but preserves history in case you reactivate later).

faq.q: Will it work with my caching plugin?
faq.a: Yes. Tested and compatible with WP Rocket, WP Super Cache, W3 Total Cache, LiteSpeed Cache, and Cloudflare Page Rules. The JS tracker loads async and needs no cache workaround.

faq.q: Can I exclude admins from tracking?
faq.a: Yes, via a toggle in Settings. For advanced control, use the PHP filter ajnix_should_track_current_user to exclude specific roles or users.
On free: Contact Form 7, WPForms, Gravity Forms, Fluent Forms. On Pro: all of the above plus Elementor Pro Forms, Ninja Forms, Forminator, Formidable Forms, WS Form, Caldera Forms.

faq.q: What's different between Free and Pro?
faq.a: Free gives you full WooCommerce tracking, last-touch attribution, custom goals (up to 5), manual marketing costs (up to 10), the 4 main form plugins, and 90 days of detailed history. Pro ($99/mo, or $49.50/mo for life for the first 100) adds the 5 other attribution models, automatic ad cost sync, server-side conversions, AI insights, real-time, unlimited history, and covers up to 3 sites with 3 team members (consolidated dashboard). For agencies or brands operating more than 3 sites, the Agency tier ($299/mo) includes unlimited sites and team members plus white-label reports.

faq.q: Can I use it on multiple sites?
faq.a: Yes. The free version installs on an unlimited number of WordPress sites (each instance is independent, with its own data, no consolidated dashboard). Pro ($99/mo) manages up to 3 sites with a single consolidated dashboard and 3 team members, ideal for brands operating multiple stores (FR/EN, B2B/B2C, multi-niches). For agencies or larger operations, the Agency tier ($299/mo) includes unlimited sites, unlimited team members, and white-label reports.

faq.q: Do I need a consent banner?
faq.a: In most regions, no. Ajnix is privacy-first by design: anonymized IP, no PII stored, no fingerprinting. You stay compliant with PIPEDA, GDPR, and CCPA by default. If you already run a consent banner (Complianz, CookieYes, Cookiebot, Iubenda), Ajnix can integrate via a dedicated option.

faq.q: How does Ajnix compare to MonsterInsights or GA4?
faq.a: MonsterInsights and GA4 focus on last-touch attribution and do not decompose true WooCommerce revenue (COGS, fees, net profit). Ajnix is designed specifically for WooCommerce with 6 attribution models, native net revenue calculation, and privacy compliance by default. See the comparison table above for details.

Yes. Ajnix works as a generic analytics and attribution tool on any WordPress site. Without WooCommerce, you lose ecommerce revenue decomposition but keep everything else: multi-touch attribution, custom goals, UTM builder, form integrations, PHP hooks.

faq.q: Can I migrate from another analytics plugin?
faq.a: Historical events from your previous plugin cannot be imported into Ajnix (data schemas differ). We recommend running the old plugin in parallel for a few weeks to compare, then fully switching to Ajnix. On Pro, all your Ajnix events are synced to our SaaS on upgrade, retroactively.

waitlist.founderTitle: Why I built Ajnix, by Éric St-Amant
waitlist.founderStory:
For years, I ran ad campaigns, worked with media buyers, and shipped conversion tracking systems for clients who wanted to understand their marketing ROI.

The pattern was always the same. Nobody really knows where their sales are coming from.

Meta systematically takes credit. Google Ads tells a different story. WooCommerce shows yet another number. The three platforms contradict each other, and the e-commerce operator in the middle ends up making budget decisions on top of fragmented data.

And most of those stores run on WordPress, an ecosystem that has never really had a serious attribution tool. WooCommerce Analytics is last-touch and stops there. GA4 lies and takes weeks to configure. Enterprise tools like Northbeam or Hyros sit around $500/mo, out of reach for most stores doing under $100k in monthly GMV.

I built Ajnix to fill that gap. Understand the real correlation between ad spend and online sales. See which channel actually drives the conversion. Answer concrete questions: do my customers discover my brand through Google Ads, then convert after a Meta retargeting hit? Which channel is the real trigger, and which one just collects the credit at the end of the journey?

Ajnix answers that, directly in WordPress, with 6 attribution models, real net revenue broken down, and privacy compliance by default. No consultants, no overengineered stacks, no $500/mo.

Built in Canada, for e-commerce operators who want to stop guessing.

faq.q: Is the WordPress plugin really free forever?
faq.a: Yes. The Ajnix WordPress plugin (last-touch attribution + full WooCommerce tracking + custom goals + manual costs) will always be free, on an unlimited number of sites (separate instances, no consolidated dashboard).

faq.q: How does the early-bird discount work?
faq.a: The first 100 people who join the Pro waitlist lock in $49.50/mo for life instead of $99/mo, and keep that rate as long as the account is active.

faq.q: What's the difference between Free and Pro?
faq.a: Free covers WooCommerce tracking, last-touch attribution, 5 goals and 10 manual costs, on unlimited but independent sites. Pro adds the 5 other attribution models, auto-import of Google Ads and Meta costs, server-side conversions, AI insights, real-time, and covers 3 sites in a consolidated dashboard with 3 team members.

Agency is designed for agencies and brands operating more than 3 sites, or needing white-label reports, advanced permissions, or extended ads sync (LinkedIn, Microsoft). Unlimited sites and team members, 4h support SLA.
Yes, upgrade or downgrade at any time. Pro-rated billing.

press.subtitle: Everything journalists, analysts and partners need to cover Ajnix. Boilerplate copy, logos, brand colors, founder bio and a direct contact for interviews.
press.boilerplateLede: Copy any of these descriptions verbatim. Please use the spelling "Ajnix" (CamelCase) in all contexts.
press.boilerplateShort: Ajnix is the first WordPress plugin that delivers real multi-touch attribution for WooCommerce. It reveals which marketing channels actually drive sales, with 6 attribution models side by side, native net-revenue calculation and privacy-first tracking. Built in Canada for e-commerce operators tired of fragmented dashboards and last-click lies.
press.boilerplateLong: Ajnix is a WordPress-native multi-touch attribution and conversion analytics platform, purpose-built for WooCommerce stores. While tools like Google Analytics rely on last-click attribution and enterprise suites like Northbeam or Hyros start at $500/month, Ajnix brings transparent, multi-touch attribution directly into the WordPress admin — free to install, privacy-first by default. The product compares six attribution models side by side (last-touch, first-touch, linear, time-decay, position-based and data-driven), calculates real net revenue including COGS and refunds, and runs without GTM, cookies banners in most regions, or consultants. Ajnix was founded in 2026 by Éric St-Amant, a long-time e-commerce operator and tracking engineer. The company is headquartered in Canada and launches in two phases: a free WordPress plugin, followed by a Pro tier with native ads sync and server-side conversions.

press.founderBio: Éric St-Amant is the founder of Ajnix. For over a decade, he has built conversion-tracking systems, worked alongside media buyers, and shipped analytics pipelines for e-commerce operators. He launched Ajnix after watching the same pattern play out on every account: marketing platforms contradicting each other, last-click lies masking real channel performance, and enterprise analytics tools priced out of reach for stores under $100k GMV/month. Ajnix is his answer — serious attribution, inside WordPress, for the operators who actually run the store.
